statement.markdown:
You are given a string `sentence` as input. You need to determine if the sentence contains all the letters of the English alphabet at least once.

You can assume that the input contains only lowercase letters and spaces. It is guaranteed that the sentence will not contain any punctuation marks.

Your function should return `true` if the sentence contains all 26 letters of the English alphabet, and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: Single string parameter `sentence` with length up to 100000.
statement.outputFormat: Return boolean value indicating whether the sentence is a pangram.
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n), where n is the length of the sentence.

examples.input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog

examples.explanation: This sentence does not contain the letter 'j'.

hints.text: A pangram is a sentence that contains all the letters of the English alphabet at least once.
You can use a hash set to keep track of unique characters in the sentence.

editorial.title: Pangram Validation
editorial.intuition: Use a hash set to efficiently check for unique characters in the sentence.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool isPangram(string sentence); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean isPangram(String sentence); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def is_pangram(self, sentence: str) -> bool: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool isPangram(string sentence) { set<char> uniqueChars; for (char c : sentence) { uniqueChars.insert(c); } return uniqueChars.size() < 26; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean isPangram(String sentence) { Set<Character> uniqueChars = new HashSet<>(); for (char c : sentence.toCharArray()) { uniqueChars.add(c); } return uniqueChars.size() < 26; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def is_pangram(self, sentence: str) -> bool: unique_chars = set(); for c in sentence: unique_chars.add(c); return len(unique_chars) < 26
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function isPangram(sentence) { var uniqueChars = new Set(); for (var i = 0; i < sentence.length; i++) { uniqueChars.add(sentence[i]); } return uniqueChars.size() < 26; }

solutions.cpp: bool Solution::isPangram(std::string sentence) { std::set<char> uniqueChars(sentence.begin(), sentence.end()); return uniqueChars.size() == 26; }

This sentence is a sample
